recalls.Description: Kimpex is recalling its Rush and Arrow II Ski Kits, along with the corresponding adaptor kits, which are designed to replace skis on snowmobiles. These kits were available in various colors including red, green, yellow, white, black, and blue. The brand name 'Kimpex' can be found stamped on both sides of the ski handles on the snowmobiles.
recalls.Title: Kimpex Recalls Snowmobile Ski and Adaptor Kits Due to Crash Risk and Serious Injury Potential
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected snowmobiles immediately and should contact Kimpex Inc. at 800-561-6461 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at service@kimpex.com or visit https://www.kimpex.com/en-us/page/safety-recalls for further information. Kimpex is directly notifying all known purchasers.

Products.Name: Kimpex Rush Ski Kit
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,980 (In addition, 2,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Kimpex Rush ski kit and adaptor kit

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the skis falling off snowmobiles. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through Kimpex USA dealers across the country and online at Kimpex.com and other sites from April 2022 to January 2025, priced between $300 and $500 for the ski kits and approximately $23 for the adaptor kits.

Distributors.Name: Kimpex (U.S.A.) Ltd., of Champlain, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of skis detaching from the snowmobile, leading to potential crashes and serious injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected snowmobiles and contact a Kimpex dealer to obtain a free repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: Kimpex recalls Rush and Arrow II Ski Kits due to crash hazards. Stop using immediately and contact Kimpex for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: Fischer Sports has issued a recall for its RC4 50, RC4 60, and RC4 65 Junior Ski Boots, available in sizes ranging from 21.5 to 27.5. The RC4 50 and RC4 60 models were offered in Rhino Grey and Snow colors, while the RC4 65 was available in yellow. Each boot features the 'Fischer' logo on the lower shell, with the model name displayed on the upper cuff between the buckles, and the size stamped on the sole.
recalls.Title: Fischer Sports Recalls RC4 Junior Ski Boots Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Fischer Sports US at 800-844-7810 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email juniorboot.us@fischersports.com, or visit https://www.fischersports.com/voluntary-product-update-junior-alpine-ski-boot-recall or www.fischersports.com and navigate to the 'Voluntary Product Update Junior Alpine Ski Boots Recall' section at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Fischer RC4 50 Junior Ski Boots
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,460 (In addition, about 114 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Fischer RC4 60 Junior Ski Boots - Snow
Recalled Fischer RC4 60 Junior Ski Boots - Rhino Grey
Recalled Fischer RC4 50 Junior Ski Boots - Rhino Grey
Recalled Fischer RC4 50 Junior Ski Boots - Snow
Recalled Fischer RC4 65 Junior Ski Boots - Yellow
Size location on bottom of ski boot

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an incident of the ski boot cuff rotated. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Fischer Sports US, of Auburn, New Hampshire

Retailers.Name: Available at ski gear stores across the country and online at https://www.fischersports.com/us_en/ and other websites from October 2022 to February 2024, priced between $200 and $275.

Importers.Name: Fischer Sports US, of Auburn, Hampshire

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to cuff rotation and lock mechanism failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled RC4 Junior Ski Boots and contact Fischer Sports for refund, replacement, or repair instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Fischer Sports recalls RC4 Junior Ski Boots due to a fall hazard. Learn how to get a refund or replacement.
